items.definition_en: A jump is when someone pushes off and goes into the air.
items.example_en: He made a big jump from the platform.

items.definition_en: A hill is a raised area of land.
items.example_en: We watched the skiers at the top of the hill.

items.definition_en: A ski is a long narrow piece of equipment used to slide on snow.
items.example_en: He put on his skis before the competition.

items.definition_en: Snow is the white frozen substance that falls from the sky in winter.
items.example_en: Fresh snow covered the ski hill.

items.definition_en: An athlete is a person who plays sports well.
items.example_en: The athlete trained every day for the big event.

items.definition_en: A competition is an event where people try to win.
items.example_en: Many teams joined the ski jumping competition.

items.definition_en: A winner is the person who comes first in a game or race.
items.example_en: The winner held the trophy above his head.

items.definition_en: A judge decides the score or result in a competition.
items.example_en: The judge gave high points for the jump.

items.definition_en: A helmet is something you wear on your head to keep it safe.
items.example_en: Every jumper must wear a helmet.

items.definition_en: A gate is a place you go through to enter or leave an area.
items.example_en: The athlete waited at the starting gate.

items.definition_en: A spectator is a person who watches an event.
items.example_en: Every spectator cheered for their favorite jumper.

items.definition_en: A landing is the moment when you come down to the ground after being in the air.
items.example_en: He made a perfect landing on the snow.
